response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack web and backend developer with a broad polyglot background, but he is fundamentally misaligned with the core requirements of this role. The position mandates 8 years of C++ programming experience; the resume shows at most 6 months of C/C++ work at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and web technologies — not systems-level C++. Additionally, the role requires 5 years of large-scale infrastructure or distributed systems experience and a formal technical leadership track, neither of which is meaningfully demonstrated; his infrastructure exposure is incidental (AWS CDK at one job, Kubernetes for an open-source side project) rather than the core of his work. He lacks a dedicated cloud compute or platform engineering background entirely.
strengths: Strong distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency, high-performance design
Consistent evidence of software craftsmanship culture: TDD advocacy, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and community teaching through workshops and dojos
Broad multi-domain experience across web, mobile, embedded, and GIS platforms demonstrating adaptability and engineering depth
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient: the minimum requirement is 8 years; the resume shows roughly 6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 with no subsequent C++ work
No formal technical leadership role: mentoring evidence is limited to community workshops and informal coding sessions, not engineering team leadership or architectural ownership

Cloud platform experience is shallow and incidental: AWS CDK for a pricing microservice and personal Kubernetes usage do not constitute 5 years of large-scale cloud infrastructure or compute platform engineering

key_factors.factor: High-throughput distributed systems work at Dunnhumby
